tool_args.label: COVID-19 Omicron Variant Impact on Ridership
tool_args.raw_text: Passenger revenue through March was below the Adopted budget forecast by $170 million (17%) in part due to the effects of the COVID-19 Omicron variant impact on subway, bus and commuter rail ridership.

payload.claim_text: Passenger revenue below budget in part due to COVID-19 Omicron variant impact on subway, bus and commuter rail ridership

tool_args.label: Overtime Due to Vacancy/Absentee Coverage
tool_args.raw_text: The bulk of the overrun was at NYCT and reflected higher costs for vacancy/absentee coverage.

payload.claim_text: NYCT overtime overrun primarily due to vacancy/absentee coverage costs

tool_args.label: Kronos Timekeeping Outage Revision Note
tool_args.raw_text: Note: Due to the Kronos timekeeping outage, overtime spending figures are subject to revision.

payload.claim_text: Due to the Kronos timekeeping outage, overtime spending figures are subject to revision

tool_args.label: Capital Lockbox Funds Restricted to Capital Purposes
tool_args.raw_text: Funds received from the Mansion Tax and Internet Sales Tax are deposited into the Capital Lockbox and are only available for capital purposes.

payload.claim_text: Funds from Mansion Tax and Internet Sales Tax deposited into Capital Lockbox and only available for capital purposes

tool_args.label: Adopted Budget Based on McKinsey Scenarios
tool_args.raw_text: The Adopted Budget, for NYCT Subway and Bus, MTA Bus, the LIRR and MNR are based on the midpoint between the "best case" and "worst case" scenarios that were developed by McKinsey & Co., while SIR is based on the "worst case" scenario and B&T reflects the "best case" scenario.

payload.claim_text: Adopted Budget uses McKinsey midpoint scenario for NYCT, MTA Bus, LIRR, MNR; worst case for SIR; best case for B&T
